name: Joanna Jedrzejczyk
winProfile: Big signature wins, lighter SW volume

qualityWinLedger.context: Elite strawweight title challenger and defining rival.
Elite strawweight contender, close first fight context.
Elite strawweight title challenger and future champion.
Ranked title challenger in early strawweight era.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Rose losses did not end window because Joanna remained elite and fought Zhang in a title-level war. Zhang I is endpoint.

lossContextEvents.context: Immediate title rematch kept Joanna championship-level.
Joanna stayed elite and later challenged Zhang for the title.

lossContextEvents.type: prime upward-division elite vacant-title decision loss

shortCase: Joanna is the strawweight standard: elite striking volume, long title control, and one of the most important early women’s UFC championship runs.
bestArgument: Joanna wins comparisons when strawweight title control, technical dominance, and division-defining impact matter most.
counterArgument: Joanna’s argument is divisional standard-setting. She may not have two-division value, but she built the strawweight benchmark.
whyRankedHere: Joanna ranks women’s #3 because her strawweight reign was historically important and technically dominant. Her title defenses, pace, takedown defense, and striking output made her the early standard for the division.
whyNotHigher: She does not pass Nunes or Valentina because she has less two-division value, less total title-fight separation, and the Rose/Zhang stretch damaged the back end of the case.
finalTakeaway: Joanna is the strawweight title standard: technically brilliant, historically important, and clearly women’s top-three in this ranking, but capped below the two deeper GOAT cases.
